behaviors.description: Required patient fields are validated before downstream processing; missing fields are reported together.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration (validator + intake orchestrator handoff). PASS2-AUDIT: Unit could test field-check logic, but behavior explicitly requires "before any downstream processing," which is an inter-module control-flow boundary. Higher levels rejected: no real OS/network/subprocess is needed if downstream collaborators are in-process spies; no real AI output dependence; no multi-agentic chain. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Wire intake orchestrator with real validator and in-process spy fakes for insurance/triage/scheduling/confirm; submit payload missing multiple required fields; assert one error lists all missing fields and assert no downstream module was invoked.

behaviors.description: DOB validation rejects future dates, >150-year-old dates, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Unit (pure validation rules). PASS2-AUDIT: No module handoff is required by this behavior; it is local parsing/range logic. Higher levels rejected by behavior text: only date constraints are specified, not infrastructure interactions. FINAL: Unit.
behaviors.test_description: Call DOB validator directly with boundary and malformed inputs (future, older-than-150-years cutoff, non-date text) and assert specific rejection outcomes.

behaviors.description: Insurance eligibility call sends correct patient ID/plan code and interprets eligible/not eligible/pending.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: System (real HTTP contract boundary to payer API). PASS2-AUDIT: Integration with mocked client can miss request serialization/header/path/query mismatches; behavior says "sends ... to the payer API," which is an HTTP boundary. Higher levels rejected: no AI output quality dependency; no multi-agentic sequence. FINAL: System.
behaviors.test_description: Run against a real local HTTP test server emulating payer API; verify received request fields (patient ID, plan code) and assert app maps response states eligible/not eligible/pending correctly.

behaviors.description: If payer API is unreachable, eligibility becomes pending and intake continues.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: System (real network failure mode). PASS2-AUDIT: A mock exception may not match real transport failures/timeouts/connection-refused behavior; behavior explicitly says "payer API is unreachable." Higher levels rejected: no model-output correctness dependency; no multiple agentic decisions. FINAL: System.
behaviors.test_description: Use real HTTP client config pointed to an unreachable endpoint (or refused port) and run intake; assert eligibility is set to pending and downstream steps still execute.

behaviors.description: Triage invocation payload includes symptoms and configured emergency rubric.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration (service + config + LLM client wrapper). PASS2-AUDIT: This behavior tests call construction, not model judgment; by text it is about "model input payload" contents. Unit is plausible if payload builder is isolated, but end-to-end assembly from config and request is a module boundary. Higher levels rejected: no need for real model output evaluation; no OS/network requirement if client is stubbed. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Run triage service with real config loader and a stub LLM client that records requests; assert payload includes reported symptoms and emergency rubric text.

behaviors.description: Live AI triage marks emergency symptom combinations as high urgency.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Agentic (depends on real model decision quality). PASS2-AUDIT: Stubbed outputs would always pass and cannot validate classification behavior for "chest pain plus shortness of breath." Workflow rejected by behavior text: single triage decision, not multiple agentic stages. FINAL: Agentic.
behaviors.test_description: Send real triage requests with emergency symptom combinations to the production-like AI engine and assert high-urgency classification with repeat-run tolerance thresholds.

behaviors.description: Triage parser stores clinician-readable explanation, not just numeric score.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration (parser + persistence handoff). PASS2-AUDIT: Unit can test parsing text extraction, but behavior includes "stores" explanation, which is cross-module state propagation. Higher levels rejected: can use deterministic triage fixture; no real model-quality requirement; no real OS/network needed beyond local wiring. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Feed deterministic triage response fixture containing score+explanation through parser and repository layer; assert persisted record includes non-empty explanation field.

behaviors.description: Live AI triage explanation is case-specific and includes rationale, not boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Agentic (output quality/content specificity). PASS2-AUDIT: Deterministic fixtures cannot reveal whether the model actually references case symptoms and urgency rationale; behavior explicitly targets live explanation quality. Workflow rejected: only one model decision stage is being assessed. FINAL: Agentic.
behaviors.test_description: Run live triage on varied symptom inputs; assert explanation text references input-specific symptoms and urgency reasoning using rubric-based quality checks/human-labeled heuristics.

behaviors.description: Scheduler picks first available slot matching urgency SLA (same-day high, <=48h medium).
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration (urgency-to-slot logic + calendar data access). PASS2-AUDIT: Pure unit could test ordering logic only, but behavior includes availability search over calendar state. System rejected: local DB/in-process repo is sufficient; no real external OS/network primitive required. Agentic/workflow rejected: no model decision dependency. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Seed local calendar store with controlled availability; run scheduling for high/medium urgencies; assert first valid slot chosen and SLA windows enforced.

behaviors.description: Concurrent intake processing does not double-book a slot.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration (concurrency + shared calendar DB transaction behavior). PASS2-AUDIT: Unit cannot reproduce race conditions across repository/locking boundaries. System considered, but behavior text says shared calendar database and can be reproduced with local DB concurrency tests without external OS/network services. Agentic/workflow disqualified: no AI decision dependency. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Use real local calendar DB with unique constraint/locking enabled; launch two concurrent scheduling requests for same slot; assert exactly one booking succeeds and no duplicate record exists.

behaviors.description: Confirmation includes name, time, provider, and visit-type-specific prep instructions.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration (appointment/provider data + template/composer). PASS2-AUDIT: Could be unit for template rendering, but behavior depends on correct field assembly from upstream appointment context and visit-type logic. Higher levels rejected: no real OS/network needed; no AI-output dependence; no multi-agentic chain. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Run confirmation composer with real appointment/context objects from scheduling output and visit type variants; assert message contains required fields and correct preparation instructions.

behaviors.description: Deterministic end-to-end orchestration with stubbed triage output completes correctly.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration (module orchestration across pipeline). PASS2-AUDIT: Behavior explicitly says "deterministic stubbed triage output," disqualifying Agentic for this behavior. System considered, but real external infra is not required if payer/triage are stubbed and calendar is local. Workflow disqualified by text because agentic decisions are stubbed out. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Execute full pipeline in-process with deterministic triage stub and controlled payer/scheduler fixtures; assert stage order, state handoff, and successful completion with expected artifacts.

behaviors.description: Full live intake pipeline completes for a real patient submission without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Agentic (live model call outcome influences downstream scheduling/confirmation). PASS2-AUDIT: System is necessary for real infra, but insufficient alone because success depends on real triage output being usable/correct for downstream flow. Workflow considered; rejected by behavior text listing one AI step ("triage") rather than multiple agentic decisions in sequence. FINAL: Agentic.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a real patient intake through production-like stack using live payer API and live triage model; assert unattended completion through validate→insurance→triage→schedule→confirm with successful persisted outputs.
